items.title: Sarah Game won't rule out introducing another bill to restrict late term abortion

children.content: Family First upper house MLC Sarah Game has not ruled out introducing another bill restricting late-term abortion access, after her proposed legislation to restrict abortion after 25 weeks failed in the lower house last night.

items.title: The grassroots club that gave Socceroo Irankunda a go

children.content: Long before Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal buoyed Australian fans he made his start with Adelaide's Northern Wolves Soccer Club, where he is a hero to many other young players who share a similar background.

items.title: Embattled National Trust of SA to front inquiry over volunteer complaints

children.content: An outpouring of discontent among National Trust volunteers in recent months has led state parliament to inquire into "accountability and oversight" concerns within the major conservation group.

items.title: Snowtown serial killer's release subject to another review

children.content: Another review will be held to decide whether Snowtown serial killer James Vlassakis should be released on parole.

items.title: Multi-million-dollar fund for SA farming help not used for a decade

children.content: A South Australian government fund of more than $28 million for loans and grants for farmers has not been used for the past 10 years, despite the industry struggling through drought and poor market conditions.

image.altText: Rows of green vineyards with wooden posts.

items.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track livestock lost in floods

children.content: Closed roads and flooded plains are making livestock inaccessible for some pastoralists, while others are employing some serious technology to track down their animals.

items.title: Experts fear algal bloom to blame for disappearance of Whyalla's cuttlefish

children.content: Whyalla is the breeding spot for hundreds of thousands of cuttlefish, but the international spectacle has been cancelled after only dozens have been spotted this year.

image.altText: Four Giant Australian cuttlefish are seen close up at a low angle under water. they are purple and grey in colour.

items.title: Couple sentenced for neglecting more than 100 pets

children.content: A South Australian couple is barred from owning pets after mistreating more than 100 animals, including cats, a dog, chickens and other birds.

image.altText: A brown dog in a cage stands in her cage next to a water bowl filled with green algae and faeces laying around her.

items.title: Injured toddler's ordeal highlights risks of weather radar blackspot

children.content: When two-year-old Teddy suffered a suspected broken leg on his family's outback property, the journey to hospital was complicated by a lack of reliable weather forecasting.

image.altText: A young boy with a cast on his leg sits on a lounge chair at home.

items.title: 'Thou shalt not kill, I have sinned': Jury hears alleged parent murder confession

children.content: Accused murderer Raelene Polymiadis was allegedly captured on police listening devices confessing to killing her elderly parents while praying, a court has heard.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder length hair and sunglasses.

items.title: The 2 minutes and 46 seconds of Hanson's speech that she thinks matters

children.content: When Pauline Hanson stood up at the National Press Club, it was not just for the audience watching on broadcast TV. In the age of social media, the bite-sized clips that come later are far more important.

articles.title: An orange bulldozer is ploughing through the political arena

children.content: Four years ago, Australians decided they didn't want a bulldozer for a prime minister. But now if Gina Rinehart has her way, a big orange one will be ploughing her way through the political arena.

image.altText: pauline hanson gina rinehart bulldozer

articles.title: Pauline Hanson's criticism of Guardian reporter labelled an 'assault on free press'

children.content: Pauline Hanson's public excoriation of a Guardian journalist questioning her at the National Press Club yesterday is an "assault on the freedom of the press", the media union says, as Labor and Liberal MPs criticise the One Nation leader for getting personal with journalists.

articles.title: Hanson's fiery address creates risk for One Nation

children.content: In one press club appearance, we've learned more about where Hanson stands. And that creates risk for One Nation.

items.title: Ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood is dead

items.title: Coroner suggests racism marred investigation of Indigenous teen's death

items.title: NT government reveals scale of bungled smart meter rollout
